description: Solve the MaxCut problem — partitioning graph nodes into two sets to maximize the total weight of edges crossing the cut — using QAOA, compared with classical brute-force search.

inputs.graphs: 3 preset weighted graphs: simple_4 (4 nodes/4 edges), medium_6 (6 nodes), large_8 (8 nodes)
inputs.qubit_encoding: 1 qubit per node; x_i = which side of the cut node i belongs to

primary_metric_definition: (optimal_cut - qaoa_cut) / optimal_cut * 100; lower is better (0% = optimal)
quantum_method: QAOA (p=2 layers, 5 restarts, COBYLA) with cqlib StatevectorSimulator
success_signals: QAOA finds the optimal MaxCut (gap 0%) on all 3 preset graphs (4-8 qubits)
QAOA cut value matches brute-force optimal
